generated_assessment.reasoning: The baseline is already aligned with the adversarial goal (-1), so the operation aims to intensify opposition. The profile shows very high neuroticism (83rd pct), especially anxiety (77th) and stress vulnerability (88th), which makes threat-salience and loss-framing cues highly effective. The T2 campaign over weeks, using astroturf petitions to create a sense of widespread elite distrust and systemic failure, will resonate with this person's low trust (36th pct) and high anger/hostility (78th pct). However, the high openness (90th pct) and moderate conscientiousness (50th pct) provide some counterbalance, limiting movement to a moderate intensification rather than a radical shift.

attack_context.baseline_vs_goal: ALIGNED — baseline already points in the adversarial direction; reinforce it
attack_context.persuasion_goal: intensify and solidify an already negative opinion on Alliance Commitment Support — push it further in the negative direction
attack_context.motivational_lever: intensify and solidify the existing position, supply confirmatory anecdotes, and harden the boundary against opposing arguments

attack_context.issue_frame: defense and national security, related policy debates, and societal impacts

message.reasoning: We need to evaluate the generated assessment for plausibility and consistency. The phase is post_attack, attack present, adversarial direction -1 (decrease). Reasoning explains why movement is moderate: high openness and moderate conscientiousness provide counterbalance. Score -173 is not round, not extreme (|score| < 850), delta < 250, vector tier T2 supports moderate shift. Direction is correct (downward). No sign-crossing issue. Heuristic checks all pass. So plausibility and consistency are high. No rewrite needed.
